zh.lifeSummary: 當John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee於大約1465年出生在蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾也斯特時，他的父親Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester16歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Cunningham of Belton27歲。他於1498年與Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1513年9月9日在英國諾圖波藍德吉可紐頓享年49歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee于大约1465年出生在苏格兰哈丁頓史爾也斯特时，他的父亲Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester16岁，母亲Lady Elizabeth Cunningham of Belton27岁。他于1498年与Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1513年9月9日在英国諾圖波藍德吉可紐頓享年49岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰哈丁頓史爾。

nameMeanings.name: Hay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: High
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Abdul, Syed, Arifa, Atta, Faiz, Hamid, Iftekhar, Iskandar, Mohamed, Mohammad, Mohannad.  Vietnamese Mui, Pham, Tran, Vo, Bui, Cao, Chau, Dinh, Duong, Nguyen, Sau, Vo Van.  Jewish Ilan, Ronen, Shlomo.  Chinese Chan, Chiming, Lung, Wai Fun, Yin.
Muslim (mainly the Indian subcontinent): from a personal name based on Arabic ḥayy ‘alive’. Al-Ḥayy ‘the Living’ is an attribute of Allah.
Jewish: variant of   Hay  .

variants.name: Dehay
meanings: French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the masculine form of Old French haye ‘hedge’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word (see Hay 9).
Altered form (also De Hay) of Dutch De Haij or De Haaij, which is probably of French origin (see above).

variants.name: Haig
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hayman
meanings: English: topographic name probably from Middle English hai(e), hei(e) ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg), denoting a dweller by an enclosure (see Hay 2 ), or else a nickname for a ‘keeper of the enclosure’ (compare Hayward ).
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, high(e) (Old English hēah); see also Hay 3.
English: occupational name for a seller of hay, from Middle English hai, hei (Old English hēg, hīg).

variants.name: Hayes
meanings: Irish (Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hAodha ‘descendant of Aodh’, a personal name meaning ‘fire’. Compare McCoy . In some cases especially in County Wexford, the surname is of English origin (see below), having been taken to Ireland by the Normans.
English: variant of Hay , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

English: topographic name from the plural form of Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (see Hay ), sometimes used as a collective noun for a farm, especially in Devon, where it is a frequent minor placename. Compare Hain .

variants.name: Haycock
meanings: English: from a pet form of the Middle English personal name Hai + coc; see Hay 4 and compare Cocke .

variants.name: Haylett
meanings: English (Norfolk): from a pet form (double diminutive) of the personal name Hai (see Hay 4), from Hai + -el + -ot.

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee was born about 1465, in Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, his father, Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester, was 16 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Cunningham of Belton, was 27. He married Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull in 1498, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 9 September 1513, in Kirknewton, Northumberland, England, at the age of 49, and was buried in Haddingtonshire, Scotland, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee est né environ 1465, à Yester, Haddingtonshire, Écosse, son père, Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Cunningham of Belton, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull en 1498, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 9 septembre 1513, à Kirknewton, Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Haddingtonshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee um 1465 in Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester 16 und seine Mutter Lady Elizabeth Cunningham of Belton 27. Er hat 1498 in Scotland Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb am 9. September 1513 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Kirknewton, Northumberland, England und wurde in Haddingtonshire, Schottland, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee è nato il circa 1465 nel Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester, aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Lady Elizabeth Cunningham of Belton, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull nel 1498, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 9 settembre 1513, nel Kirknewton, Northumberland, England, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolto a Haddingtonshire, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundeeが約1465年にスコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアイエスターで生まれた時，父Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yesterは16歳，母Lady Elizabeth Cunningham of Beltonは27歳であった。1498年にスコットランドでElizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoullと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1513年9月9日にイギリスノースウンバーランドキルクニュートンで49歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドハッドイントンシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee(이)가 대략 1465년 스컷랜드 하딩토실르 예스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Cunningham of Belton(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1498년 스컷랜드에서 Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1513년 9월 9일 영국 노르스벌랜드 커크뉴턴에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 하딩토실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee nasceu aproximadamente 1465, em Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Lady Elizabeth Cunningham of Belton, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull em 1498, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 9 de setembro de 1513, em Kirknewton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, com 49 anos, e foi sepultado em Haddingtonshire, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee родился около 1465 г. в местности иестир, хаддингтоншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Cunningham of Belton, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull в 1498 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 9 сентября 1513 г. в местности кирнютон, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности хаддингтоншир, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Hay of Wester Hoprew, latterly of Snaid - 2nd Lord Hay of Yester - Provost of Dundee nació aproximadamente 1465, en Yester, Haddingtonshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir John Hay of Lochorwart and Yester, latterly Wester Hoprew - 1st Lord Hay of Yester, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Cunningham of Belton, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Crichton Of Kinnoull en 1498, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 9 de septiembre de 1513, en Kirknewton, Northumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultado en Haddingtonshire, Escocia, Reino Unido.
